title: Chugaev Elimination Reaction (Xanthate Ester Pyrolysis) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Chugaev elimination reaction describing pyrolytic syn elimination of alkyl xanthates to alkenes through a six-membered cyclic transition state, typically used to dehydrate sensitive alcohols without skeletal rearrangement.
notes: Key concepts: xanthate ester formation from alcohols, cis/syn elimination, thermal pyrolysis to alkene.

title: Chugaev Elimination Reaction (Xanthate Ester Pyrolysis) – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Chugaev elimination reaction featuring late-stage exocyclic alkene installation in dihydroclerodin, selective double-bond generation in solanapyrone E synthesis, ellacene formation, and tetrahydrofluorenone construction en route to kinamycins.
notes: Examples emphasize xanthate pyrolysis as a reliable dehydration step in complex natural product synthesis.

title: Ciamician-Dennstedt Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Ciamician-Dennstedt rearrangement describing conversion of pyrroles or indoles into 3-halopyridines or 3-haloquinolines through dihalocarbene insertion, ring expansion, and dehydrohalogenation; also known as the abnormal Reimer-Tiemann reaction.
notes: Key concepts: dihalocarbene generation from haloforms, pyrrole ring expansion, 3-halopyridine formation.

title: Ciamician-Dennstedt Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Ciamician-Dennstedt rearrangement featuring calixpyridine assembly from calixpyrroles and solvent-dependent dichlorocarbene insertion into substituted indoles to access chloroquinoline and dichloromethyl indole products.
notes: Examples highlight macrocyclic ring expansion and product-divergent indole carbene insertion chemistry.

title: Claisen Condensation / Claisen Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
family_names: Claisen Condensation / Claisen Reaction|Claisen Condensation|Claisen Reaction
summary: Overview page for the Claisen condensation describing base-mediated ester enolate acylation to give beta-keto esters, including crossed Claisen condensation and Dieckmann cyclization variants.
notes: Key concepts: ester enolate generation, beta-keto ester formation, crossed Claisen and Dieckmann variants.

title: Claisen Condensation / Claisen Reaction – Synthetic Applications
family_names: Claisen Condensation / Claisen Reaction|Claisen Condensation|Claisen Reaction
summary: Application page for the Claisen condensation featuring convergent synthesis of secodaphniphylline, annulation en route to justicidin B and retrojusticidin B, and Evans-type stereoselective aldol/Claisen sequences toward methyl-7-benzoylpederate.
notes: Examples show mixed Claisen condensation and stereocontrolled beta-keto ester assembly in natural product settings.

title: Claisen Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Claisen rearrangement describing thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allyl vinyl ethers and allyl aryl ethers to gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyls or ortho/para-allyl phenols via concerted suprafacial transition states.
notes: Key concepts: allyl vinyl ether rearrangement, allyl aryl ether rearrangement, six-membered chairlike transition states.

title: Claisen Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Claisen rearrangement featuring Tebbe-generated allyl vinyl ether rearrangement in sclerophytin synthesis, Lewis-acid-mediated stereoselective Claisen rearrangement in saudin synthesis, and biomimetic double Claisen/Diels-Alder cascades to 1-O-methylforbesione.
notes: Applications emphasize allyl vinyl ether formation followed by thermally or Lewis-acid controlled [3,3]-rearrangement.

title: Claisen-Ireland Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
family_names: Claisen-Ireland Rearrangement|Ireland-Claisen Rearrangement|ester enolate Claisen rearrangement
summary: Overview page for the Claisen-Ireland rearrangement describing [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of O-silyl ketene acetals derived from allylic esters to furnish gamma,delta-unsaturated carboxylic acids with strong anti/syn stereocontrol depending on enolate geometry.
notes: Key concepts: allylic ester enolate, O-silyl ketene acetal, chairlike or boatlike transition state, anti/syn control.

title: Claisen-Ireland Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
family_names: Claisen-Ireland Rearrangement|Ireland-Claisen Rearrangement|ester enolate Claisen rearrangement
summary: Application page for the Claisen-Ireland rearrangement featuring ebelactone A/B stereocenter construction, convergent entry to aspidophytine, and trichodiene synthesis through highly diastereoselective rearrangement of O-silyl ketene acetals.
notes: Examples highlight stereocontrolled carbon-oxygen to carbon-carbon bond reorganization in complex settings.
